analysis.summary: This file implements the core training loop for a learner process in a distributed reinforcement learning setup. It initializes the distributed environment, loads a learner model (and optionally a frozen teacher model), and configures an optimizer with a custom learning rate scheduler. The main loop dynamically selects batch types based on probability borders, retrieves pre-processed batches from shared queues, and passes them to a `learn` function along with multiple loss handlers. It also periodically syncs updated weights back to the actor model to maintain policy consistency across processes.
analysis.competition_flow: Initializes a distributed learner process, loads models and optimizers, fetches RL batches from shared queues, performs gradient updates using multiple loss functions, and periodically syncs weights to the actor model.
insights: Uses probability-based borders to dynamically select batch types for mixed training objectives.
Periodically syncs learner weights to the actor model to maintain policy consistency across processes.
Leverages `torch.compile` and DDP with static graphs to optimize distributed RL training performance.
